text: All candidates must provide one answer for each question from the choice of a or b. You therefore need to provide six answers in total.
Where appropriate (e.g. for formulae, equations, or diagrams) you may hand write or draw on the Inspera paper provided. You should not copy and paste images from any other documents.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Discuss the similarities and differences of neuromuscular blocking agents.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Compare and contrast the different pharmacological interventions for infertility.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Discuss the role of protein phosphorylation and other post-translational modifications in Growth Factor signalling.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: The initial small molecule hits in a typical drug discovery initiative often need to be further optimised – discuss.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Compare and contrast the mechanisms of action of antiviral drugs.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Discuss the mechanisms of action of antimalarial drugs.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: With reference to specific examples, discuss how the synthesis and signalling of lipid mediators derived from arachidonic acid can be targeted to treat disease.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Using specific examples, discuss the mechanisms of action of different immunosuppressive drugs.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Compare and contrast the therapies used to treat chronic heart failure.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Compare and contrast the mechanisms of action of drugs that target voltage-gated Na+ channels and voltage-gated Ca2+ channels in treating diseases.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Compare the mechanisms of action of drugs targeting neurotransmitter reuptake transporters versus neurotransmitter receptors for treating neurological disorders.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Compare how drugs target serotonergic versus dopaminergic signalling to treat neurological disorders.
